incidents.headline: CISA Urges SharePoint Hardening After New Exploitations
incidents.summary: CISA is aware of active exploitation of vulnerabilities enabling cyber threat actors to gain unauthorized access to on-premises SharePoint Server instances.

incidents.headline: Rockwell Automation 1715-AENTR EtherNet/IP Adapter Vulnerability
incidents.summary: A vulnerability in Rockwell Automation 1715-AENTR EtherNet/IP Adapter versions could allow an attacker to read or delete files, stop tasks, modify memory, and change I/O states.

vulnerabilities.product: Schneider Electric Easergy MiCOM Px40 Series
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability in the Easergy MiCOM Px40 Series products that could allow unauthorized exposure of basic device identification through the SNMP protocol.

Vulnerabilities in the Schneider Electric PowerChute Serial Shutdown that could allow attackers to overwrite critical files, forge or inject malicious log data, gain unauthorized account access, trigger denial-of-service conditions, truncate or alter logging information, reset user credentials, or expose sensitive information.

incidents.headline: LabubaRAT malware infiltrates Windows systems while posing as NVIDIA software
incidents.summary: LabubaRAT, a Rust-based remote access tool (RAT) masquerading as NVIDIA software, has been discovered by Blackpoint Cyber. It can profile the host, identify installed security tools, receive operator commands, transfer files, capture screenshots, and proxy network traffic.

actors.name: LabubaRAT
actors.recent_activity: Infiltrating Windows systems by posing as NVIDIA software.

hunting_indicators.title: LabubaRAT Indicators
hunting_indicators.context: Remote Access Tool (RAT) masquerading as NVIDIA software.

incidents.headline: Threat actor impersonated hundreds of brands on GitHub to push infostealer malware
incidents.summary: A financially motivated threat actor is impersonating hundreds of brands on GitHub to distribute a smash-and-grab infostealer. The repositories span various categories including security tools, fintech, cryptocurrency wallets, developer and productivity tools, secure email providers, macOS utilities, and gaming software.

actors.name: Threat actor impersonating brands on GitHub
actors.recent_activity: Impersonating hundreds of brands to distribute infostealer malware.

hunting_indicators.title: Infostealer Malware Indicators on GitHub
hunting_indicators.context: Impersonating brands to distribute infostealer malware.

incidents.headline: Spanish police dismantle €140 million cybercrime network
incidents.summary: Spanish National Police have dismantled a cybercrime network accused of stealing and laundering about €140 million through fake investment platforms, CEO fraud, invoice fraud, and man-in-the-middle attacks. Four people were arrested as part of the operation.

incidents.headline: Binary Defense’s NightBeacon CMD helps enterprise SOC teams automate threat investigations
incidents.summary: Binary Defense has announced NightBeacon CMD, a standalone AI-driven SOC workbench that gives customers the same operating platform Binary Defense's own analysts use every day.

incidents.headline: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools
incidents.summary: Bitdefender researchers have demonstrated how Windows bind links can be used to create conflicting filesystem views, thereby hiding malware from endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools.

vulnerabilities.description: A critical security defect in the ServiceNow AI platform could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

incidents.headline: TuxBot v3: Inside an IoT Botnet Framework With LLM-Assisted Development
incidents.summary: An analysis of TuxBot v3, an IoT botnet framework developed with the assistance of LLMs. The report covers cross-compiled binaries and C2 architecture.

incidents.headline: Winning 54% of the time
incidents.summary: Hazel argues against the myth that attackers only need to be right once, while defenders must always be correct.

incidents.headline: OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps
incidents.summary: A malware framework called OkoBot has been operating since April 2025, targeting hardware wallet owners by injecting seed phrase phishing into Ledger and Trezor apps.

actors.name: OkoBot
actors.recent_activity: Running since April 2025, targeting hardware wallet owners with seed phrase phishing.
